cards.back: OSPF neighbor progression: Down → Init → 2-Way → ExStart → Exchange → Loading → Full. **Stuck in ExStart = MTU mismatch.** Non-DR/BDR routers stop at 2-Way on multi-access. Init = Hello received but our Router-ID not yet in their Hello list.

cards.front: Which state in this diagram is the **most common stuck point**, and what causes it?
cards.back: ExStart — caused by MTU mismatch on the interface. Fix: match MTU on both ends OR `ip mtu-ignore` on the interface.
Down → Attempt → Init → 2-Way → ExStart → Exchange → Loading → Full
Received Hello from neighbor, but neighbor did not see our Router ID in its Hello yet (one-way)

cards.front: Received Hello from neighbor, but neighbor did not see our Router ID in its Hello yet (one-way)

cards.back: Bidirectional Hello — both routers see each other. On multi-access this is where non-DR/BDR routers stay.

cards.front: Bidirectional Hello — both routers see each other. On multi-access this is where non-DR/BDR routers stay.

cards.back: Master/slave negotiation for DBD exchange. Stuck here = MTU mismatch.

cards.front: Master/slave negotiation for DBD exchange. Stuck here = MTU mismatch.

cards.back: LSR/LSU exchange — requesting full LSAs based on DBD

cards.front: LSR/LSU exchange — requesting full LSAs based on DBD
Stuck in EXSTART/EXCHANGE — most common cause?

cards.back: Stuck in EXSTART/EXCHANGE — most common cause?

cards.front: 8 things that must match for OSPF neighbors to form
cards.back:
1. Same area number
2. Hello + Dead timer values
3. Same subnet (mask + network)
4. Same network type
5. Same authentication (type + key)
6. Same MTU (for EXSTART → Full)
7. Unique Router IDs
8. Interface not passive-interface

cards.front: Verify interface in OSPF + area + timers
cards.back: `show ip ospf interface brief` or `show ip ospf interface g0/0`

Make all interfaces passive by default
cards.back: `passive-interface default` then `no passive-interface g0/0` on exceptions

cards.front: Manually set OSPF cost on interface
ASBR injects default route into OSPF

cards.back: Advertises default even if ASBR has no default in its own routing table

cards.front: Advertises default even if ASBR has no default in its own routing table
If neighbor not Full — diagnostic order
cards.back:
1. `show ip ospf neighbor` — what state?
2. `show ip ospf interface brief` — interface in OSPF, right area, timers?
3. `show run | section ospf` — network statements, router-id, auth
4. Check MTU + subnet match on both ends
5. `debug ip ospf adj` if still stuck

cards.front: OSPF neighbor stuck in INIT — cause
cards.back: Hello received but Router-ID mismatch in Hello list. Often Router-ID conflict or passive-interface one side.

cards.front: Hello received but Router-ID mismatch in Hello list. Often Router-ID conflict or passive-interface one side.
cards.back: OSPF neighbor stuck in INIT — cause

cards.front: OSPF neighbor stuck in EXSTART — cause
cards.back: MTU mismatch. Fix: `ip mtu-ignore` on interface OR match MTU.

cards.front: MTU mismatch. Fix: `ip mtu-ignore` on interface OR match MTU.
cards.back: OSPF neighbor stuck in EXSTART — cause
Wrong area, wrong subnet/mask, passive-interface, or interface not in `network` statement

Network shows in `show run` but not `show ip route ospf` — cause
cards.back: Neighbor never reached Full state. Check `show ip ospf neighbor`.

cards.front: Neighbor never reached Full state. Check `show ip ospf neighbor`.
cards.back: Network shows in `show run` but not `show ip route ospf` — cause
`do show ip ospf nei` from config mode
